_note: 36 cases. setup = inverse of primary algorithm. All cases target FR slot, white bottom, yellow top. Removed: f2l-06/07 (duplicates of f2l-01/02), f2l-41 (impossible), f2l-03/04 (AUF variants — same geometry as f2l-01/02, different starting angle; now explained in about.html). Category corner-in-top = corner free in top layer, edge in middle slot. Algorithms manually reviewed 2026-04-25.

cases.name: Pair formed — insert right
cases.setup: R U R' U'
cases.recognition:
FIND: Look in the top layer for a corner-edge pair that is already touching — the non-white color on the corner faces the same direction as the matching color on the edge.
WHITE: White sticker is on the right face of the corner (facing R), the pair is ready to slide into the slot.
EDGE: Edge is directly adjacent to the corner, colors aligned.
CONFIRM: AUF (any number of U turns) to align the pair above the FR slot, then R U' R' inserts it. The number of AUF turns is not a separate case — just rotate U until the pair is above the slot.

algorithms.label: Main
algorithms.moves: U R U' R'
algorithms.notes: U = AUF to align; R U' R' = insertion trigger

cases.name: Pair formed — insert left
cases.setup: F' U' F U
cases.recognition:
FIND: Pair is formed in the top layer — corner and edge are touching with colors aligned.
WHITE: White sticker faces the front (F face) on the corner.
EDGE: Edge is adjacent, colors matched.
CONFIRM: AUF to align the pair above the FR slot, then F' U F inserts it. Mirror of f2l-01.

algorithms.label: Main
algorithms.moves: U' F' U F
algorithms.notes: U' = AUF to align; F' U F = insertion trigger

cases.name: Both in top — white up, edge unflipped
cases.setup: R U' R' U R U2 R'
cases.recognition:
FIND: Look at the top layer — find the corner with white facing UP (on the U face, pointing at the ceiling).
WHITE: White sticker is on top. Both the R and F colors of the corner are visible on the side faces.
EDGE: Find the edge with your two slot colors. Look at the UF or UR position — the non-white edge colors face SIDEWAYS (toward F and R faces), not up. If one edge color faces up instead, you have f2l-08.
CONFIRM: Corner white-up + edge unflipped = f2l-05.
cases.shared_algorithm_note: Same algorithm as f2l-23 (corner-in-slot). Different starting positions converge to the same intermediate state.

algorithms.label: Main
algorithms.moves: R U2 R' U' R U R'

algorithms.label: Alt
algorithms.moves: F' U2 F U F' U' F
algorithms.notes: Mirror — use when approaching from F side

cases.name: Both in top — white right, edge unflipped
cases.setup: R U' R'
cases.recognition:
FIND: Find the corner with white facing RIGHT (toward the R face, visible from the right side).
WHITE: White sticker faces the R face. The non-white colors of the corner face U and F.
EDGE: Edge is in the top layer with non-white colors facing sideways (toward F and R), not up. Edge is NOT flipped. If the edge is flipped, you have f2l-09.
CONFIRM: Corner white-right + edge unflipped above FR = f2l-06. Single trigger solves it.

algorithms.label: Main
algorithms.moves: U R U' R'

cases.name: Both in top — white front, edge unflipped
cases.setup: F' U F
cases.recognition:
FIND: Find the corner with white facing FRONT (toward the F face, visible from the front).
WHITE: White sticker faces F. The non-white colors face U and R.
EDGE: Edge in top layer, non-white colors facing sideways. Not flipped. If flipped, you have f2l-10.
CONFIRM: Corner white-front + edge unflipped = f2l-07. Mirror of f2l-06.

algorithms.label: Main
algorithms.moves: U' F' U F

cases.name: Both in top — white up, edge flipped
cases.setup: R U R' U2 R U' R'
cases.recognition:
FIND: Find the corner with white facing UP — same starting point as f2l-05.
WHITE: White sticker on top, identical to f2l-05 so far.
EDGE: Find the matching edge. Now look at its top-layer position — one of the edge's non-white colors is facing UP (toward U face) instead of sideways. This is the flip. Practical test: if you can see your slot color on the top face of the edge sticker, the edge is flipped.
CONFIRM: Corner white-up + edge FLIPPED = f2l-08. One more move than f2l-05.

algorithms.label: Main
algorithms.moves: R U R' U2 R U' R'

algorithms.label: Alt
algorithms.moves: F' U' F U2 F' U F

cases.name: Both in top — white right, edge flipped
cases.setup: R U R' U' R U2 R' U
cases.recognition:
FIND: Find the corner with white facing RIGHT.
WHITE: White sticker faces R, same as f2l-06 so far.
EDGE: Find the matching edge in the top layer — one of its non-white colors faces UP (toward U face). The edge is flipped. If the edge were not flipped, you would have f2l-06.
CONFIRM: Corner white-right + edge FLIPPED = f2l-09. Longer algorithm needed to pair correctly.
cases.shared_algorithm_note: Same algorithm as f2l-24 (corner-in-slot). Different starting positions converge to the same intermediate state.

algorithms.label: Main
algorithms.moves: U' R U2 R' U R U' R'

cases.name: Both in top — white front, edge flipped
cases.setup: F' U' F U F' U2 F U'
cases.recognition:
FIND: Find the corner with white facing FRONT.
WHITE: White sticker faces F, same as f2l-07 so far.
EDGE: One of the edge's non-white colors faces UP. The edge is flipped. If unflipped, that's f2l-07.
CONFIRM: Corner white-front + edge FLIPPED = f2l-10. Mirror of f2l-09.

algorithms.label: Main
algorithms.moves: U F' U2 F U' F' U F

cases.name: Both in top — white up, pair at wrong slot
cases.setup: R U R' U' R U2 R' U'
cases.recognition:
FIND: Corner white up, edge unflipped — looks like f2l-05 at first.
WHITE: White on top.
EDGE: Edge is in the top layer, unflipped.
CONFIRM: Check which slot the pair is above. If the non-white color on the corner's R face matches the center color of the face it's above — but it's NOT the FR slot — AUF first to bring it over FR, then execute. The AUF changes the algorithm.

algorithms.label: Main
algorithms.moves: U R U2 R' U R U' R'

cases.name: Both in top — white right, edge adjacent
cases.setup: R U R' U' R U R'
cases.recognition:
FIND: Corner white facing right. Edge is in the top layer one position clockwise from the corner.
WHITE: White faces R.
EDGE: Edge's non-white colors face sideways but the edge is not directly above the FR slot — it's offset by one U move. The pieces are close but not paired.
CONFIRM: Corner white-right + edge one position away (not directly above slot) = f2l-12.
cases.shared_algorithm_note: Same algorithm as f2l-20 and f2l-31. Different starting positions converge to the same intermediate state mid-sequence.

algorithms.label: Main
algorithms.moves: R U' R' U R U' R'

cases.name: Both in top — white front, edge adjacent
cases.setup: F' U' F U F' U' F
cases.recognition:
FIND: Corner white facing front. Edge is one position counter-clockwise from the corner in the top layer.
WHITE: White faces F.
EDGE: Non-white colors face sideways, edge offset from the slot. Mirror of f2l-12.
CONFIRM: Corner white-front + edge adjacent = f2l-13.
cases.shared_algorithm_note: Same algorithm as f2l-32. Mirror pair with f2l-12.

algorithms.label: Main
algorithms.moves: F' U F U' F' U F

cases.name: Both in top — white up, edge at back
cases.setup: R U R' U2 R U2 R' U'
cases.recognition:
FIND: Corner white up. Now look for the edge — it is at the back of the U layer (UB position), unflipped.
WHITE: White on top.
EDGE: Edge is at the back of the top layer, non-white colors facing sideways. Two U moves away from direct alignment.
CONFIRM: Corner white-up + edge at back, unflipped = f2l-14.

algorithms.label: Main
algorithms.moves: U' R U2 R' U2 R U' R'

cases.name: Both in top — white up, edge flipped diagonal
cases.setup: F' U' F U2 R U R' U'
cases.recognition:
FIND: Corner white up. Edge is flipped AND positioned diagonally away from the corner.
WHITE: White on top.
EDGE: One non-white edge color faces UP. Edge is on the opposite side of the U layer from the corner (diagonal position).
CONFIRM: Corner white-up + edge flipped + diagonal = f2l-15. Requires a mixed R/F trigger sequence.

algorithms.label: Main
algorithms.moves: U R U' R' U2 F' U F

cases.name: Both in top — white up, awkward diagonal
cases.setup: R U R' U2 R U2 R'
cases.recognition:
FIND: Corner white up. Edge is on the opposite side of the top layer, unflipped.
WHITE: White on top.
EDGE: Unflipped, but the corner and edge are on opposite sides — maximum distance apart in the top layer.
CONFIRM: Corner white-up + edge directly opposite (diagonal), unflipped = f2l-16.

algorithms.label: Main
algorithms.moves: R U2 R' U2 R U' R'

cases.name: Both in top — corner FR, edge FL
cases.setup: R U' R' U F' U F
cases.recognition:
FIND: Corner is above the FR slot. Edge is above the FL slot (front-left) — they are not directly adjacent.
WHITE: Can be any orientation — the key identifier here is the POSITIONS, not just the white sticker.
EDGE: Edge is at FL position, one U away from adjacent.
CONFIRM: Corner above FR + edge above FL = f2l-17.

algorithms.label: Main
algorithms.moves: F' U' F U' R U R'

cases.name: Both in top — corner BR, edge FR
cases.setup: F' U' F U R U' R'
cases.recognition:
FIND: Corner is above the BR slot (back-right). Edge is above the FR slot.
WHITE: Position-based identification — check where the corner sits above the cube.
EDGE: Edge is at FR, corner is at BR.
CONFIRM: Corner above BR + edge above FR = f2l-18. Longer pairing path.

algorithms.label: Main
algorithms.moves: R U' R' U' F' U F

cases.name: Both in top — white up, edge far left
cases.setup: R U R' U2 F' U' F U'
cases.recognition:
FIND: Corner white up above FR slot. Edge is on the far left of the top layer (UL or UB-L position), unflipped.
WHITE: White on top above FR.
EDGE: Unflipped, but needs multiple U moves to align — maximum angular distance from the corner.
CONFIRM: Corner white-up above FR + edge at far left = f2l-19.

algorithms.label: Main
algorithms.moves: U F' U F U2 R U' R'

cases.name: Corner in slot correct — edge in top, unflipped
cases.setup: R U R' U' R U R'
cases.recognition:
FIND: Look at the FR slot — is the corner inside it with white on the bottom (D face)?
WHITE: White is HIDDEN inside the slot on the D face. You cannot see it from the top.
EDGE: Find the matching edge in the top layer. Its non-white colors should face sideways (toward F and R faces), not up. If one color faces up, the edge is flipped — see f2l-21.
CONFIRM: Corner in slot white-down + edge unflipped in top = f2l-20.
cases.shared_algorithm_note: Same algorithm as f2l-12 and f2l-31. Different starting positions converge to the same intermediate state.

algorithms.label: Main
algorithms.moves: R U' R' U R U' R'
algorithms.notes: Extract corner, pair with edge, reinsert

cases.name: Corner in slot correct — edge in top, flipped
cases.setup: R U' R' U R U' R'
cases.recognition:
FIND: Corner is in the FR slot with white hidden on D face — same as f2l-20 to start.
WHITE: White hidden in slot.
EDGE: Find the matching edge in the top layer — one of its non-white colors faces UP. The edge is flipped. If unflipped, that is f2l-20.
CONFIRM: Corner in slot white-down + edge FLIPPED in top = f2l-21.

algorithms.label: Main
algorithms.moves: R U R' U' R U R'

cases.name: Corner in slot misoriented — edge in top, unflipped
cases.setup: R U R' U' R U2 R'
cases.recognition:
FIND: Look at the FR slot. Is there a corner in it, but white is NOT on the bottom — it's on the front or right face instead?
WHITE: White sticker is visible on the F or R face of the slot (not on D). The corner is in the wrong orientation.
EDGE: Find the matching edge in the top layer — non-white colors facing sideways, not up. Unflipped.
CONFIRM: Corner in slot misoriented + edge unflipped in top = f2l-22.

algorithms.label: Main
algorithms.moves: R U2 R' U R U' R'

cases.name: Corner in slot misoriented — edge in top, flipped
cases.setup: R U' R' U R U2 R'
cases.recognition:
FIND: Corner in FR slot, white visible on F or R face (misoriented).
WHITE: White faces F or R — visible from the side, not on D.
EDGE: Matching edge in top layer, one color facing UP. Flipped. If unflipped, that is f2l-22.
CONFIRM: Corner in slot misoriented + edge FLIPPED in top = f2l-23.
cases.shared_algorithm_note: Same algorithm as f2l-05. Different starting positions converge to the same intermediate state.

algorithms.label: Main
algorithms.moves: R U2 R' U' R U R'

cases.name: Corner in slot — edge at back-right, unflipped
cases.setup: R U R' U' R U2 R' U'
cases.recognition:
FIND: Corner is in the FR slot (any orientation). Edge is in the top layer at the UR position (back-right of the U layer).
WHITE: Check the slot — corner is in there.
EDGE: Edge is at UR (back-right top position), non-white colors sideways, not up. Unflipped.
CONFIRM: Corner in slot + edge at UR, unflipped = f2l-24. AUF before the trigger.
cases.shared_algorithm_note: Same algorithm as f2l-09. Different starting positions converge to the same intermediate state.

algorithms.label: Main
algorithms.moves: U R U2 R' U R U' R'

cases.name: Corner in slot — edge at front-left, unflipped
cases.setup: F' U' F U F' U2 F U'
cases.recognition:
FIND: Corner in FR slot. Edge is at the UL position (front-left of the top layer).
WHITE: Corner in slot.
EDGE: Edge at UL (front-left top), unflipped. Mirror of f2l-24.
CONFIRM: Corner in slot + edge at UL, unflipped = f2l-25.

algorithms.label: Main
algorithms.moves: U' F' U2 F U' F' U F

cases.name: Corner in slot — edge at back-right, needs U2
cases.setup: R U' R' U R U2 R' U'
cases.recognition:
FIND: Corner in FR slot. Edge is in the top layer at UR position.
WHITE: Corner in slot.
EDGE: Edge at UR, unflipped — same position as f2l-24. The difference is the corner's orientation in the slot changes the algorithm's AUF. If the algorithm needs U at the start and U2 mid-sequence, this is f2l-26; if it needs U at the start only, it's f2l-24.
CONFIRM: Variant of f2l-24 with different corner orientation.

algorithms.label: Main
algorithms.moves: U R U2 R' U' R U R'

cases.name: Corner in slot — edge at front-left, needs U2
cases.setup: F' U F U' F' U2 F U'
cases.recognition:
FIND: Corner in FR slot. Edge is at UL (front-left top layer).
WHITE: Corner in slot.
EDGE: Edge at UL, unflipped. Mirror of f2l-26.
CONFIRM: Corner in slot + edge at UL, requires U2 mid-sequence = f2l-27.

algorithms.label: Main
algorithms.moves: U' F' U2 F U F' U' F

cases.name: Corner in top — edge in slot, correct
cases.setup: R U R'
cases.recognition:
FIND: Check the FR middle slot first. Is the edge already there? Look at the sticker on the F face of the slot — it should match the F center color. Look at the R face of the slot — should match R center.
EDGE: Edge is in the correct position AND correctly oriented — both colors match their face centers.
CONFIRM: Now find the corner anywhere in the top layer. This is the fastest F2L case: only 3 moves. The AUF is built into the algorithm for any corner position.

algorithms.label: Main
algorithms.moves: R U' R'

cases.name: Corner in top — edge in slot, flipped
cases.setup: F' U' F
cases.recognition:
FIND: Check the FR middle slot. Is the edge in it, but the sticker on the F face does NOT match the F center? Instead, the R-colored sticker is on the F face, and the F-colored sticker is on the R face — the edge is flipped.
EDGE: In the correct slot position but wrong orientation — colors are swapped between F and R faces.
CONFIRM: Edge in slot flipped + corner anywhere in top = f2l-29. Only 3 moves, mirror of f2l-28.

algorithms.label: Main
algorithms.moves: F' U F

cases.name: Corner in top — edge in adjacent wrong slot
cases.setup: F' U' F U R U' R' U'
cases.recognition:
FIND: Check the FR slot — is it empty? Now check the adjacent slots (FL or BR). Is the matching edge in one of them, correctly oriented (colors matching their face centers)?
EDGE: Edge is in a middle slot that is NOT the FR slot, but is correctly oriented within that wrong slot.
CONFIRM: Corner free in top + edge in wrong slot (oriented correctly) = f2l-30.

algorithms.label: Main
algorithms.moves: U R U' R' U' F' U F

cases.name: Corner in top white right — edge in wrong slot
cases.setup: R U R' U' R U R'
cases.recognition:
FIND: Corner is in the top layer with white facing RIGHT. Check middle slots — edge is in one of them but not FR.
WHITE: White sticker faces the R face on the corner.
EDGE: In a wrong middle slot.
CONFIRM: Corner white-right in top + edge in adjacent wrong slot = f2l-31.
cases.shared_algorithm_note: Same algorithm as f2l-12 and f2l-20. Different starting positions converge to the same intermediate state.

algorithms.label: Main
algorithms.moves: R U' R' U R U' R'

cases.name: Corner in top white front — edge in wrong slot
cases.setup: F' U' F U F' U' F
cases.recognition:
FIND: Corner in top layer with white facing FRONT. Edge is in a wrong middle slot.
WHITE: White faces F.
EDGE: In an adjacent wrong slot. Mirror of f2l-31.
CONFIRM: Corner white-front in top + edge in wrong slot = f2l-32.
cases.shared_algorithm_note: Same algorithm as f2l-13. Mirror pair with f2l-31.

algorithms.label: Main
algorithms.moves: F' U F U' F' U F

cases.name: Corner in top — edge in wrong slot, flipped
cases.setup: R U R' U R U' R' U R U2 R'
cases.recognition:
FIND: Corner is in the top layer. Check adjacent middle slots — edge is in one of them, but the sticker on the face does NOT match the face center (edge is flipped within the wrong slot).
EDGE: In wrong slot AND flipped — both position and orientation are incorrect.
CONFIRM: Corner in top + edge in wrong slot + flipped = f2l-33. The 11-move algorithm handles extraction, flip, and insertion. Alternatively, extract the edge first with a 4-move trigger, then solve the resulting simpler case.

algorithms.label: Main
algorithms.moves: R U2 R' U' R U R' U' R U' R'
algorithms.notes: Consider: extract edge first, then solve as corner-in-top case

cases.name: Corner in top — edge in BR slot
cases.setup: R U R' U' R U' R' U
cases.recognition:
FIND: Corner is free in the top layer. Check the back-right (BR) middle slot — is the matching edge there?
EDGE: Edge is in the BR slot (not FR). AUF first to bring the corner to the right position, then extract edge and insert pair.
CONFIRM: Corner in top + edge in BR slot = f2l-34.

algorithms.label: Main
algorithms.moves: U' R U R' U R U' R'

cases.name: Corner in top — edge in BR slot, flipped
cases.setup: R U R' U2 R U R'
cases.recognition:
FIND: Corner in top layer. Check the BR middle slot — edge is there, but the sticker on the R face does NOT match the R center (it's flipped within the BR slot).
EDGE: In BR slot but flipped — the R-face sticker has the back color, not R color.
CONFIRM: Corner in top + edge in BR slot + flipped = f2l-35.

algorithms.label: Main
algorithms.moves: R U' R' U2 R U' R'

cases.name: Advanced — corner correct, edge in adjacent slot
cases.setup: R U R' U R U' R' U R U R'
cases.recognition:
FIND: The FR slot looks solved — corner is in with white on the bottom. But the FR edge is missing from the slot. Instead, find the matching edge in an adjacent middle slot (FL or BR).
WHITE: Corner correctly placed, white on D.
EDGE: In a different middle slot (not FR). Both pieces are in middle layer slots but not together.
CONFIRM: Corner in correct slot + edge in adjacent wrong slot = f2l-36.

algorithms.label: Main
algorithms.moves: R U' R' U' R U R' U' R U' R'

cases.name: Advanced — corner misoriented, edge in adjacent slot
cases.setup: R U R' U' R U R' U' R U' R'
cases.recognition:
FIND: The FR slot has a corner in it, but white is NOT on the bottom — it's on the F or R face. AND the matching edge is in a different middle slot.
WHITE: Visible on the F or R face of the slot — misoriented.
EDGE: In an adjacent wrong slot. Both pieces need extracting and correcting.
CONFIRM: Corner in slot misoriented + edge in adjacent wrong slot = f2l-37.

algorithms.label: Main
algorithms.moves: R U R' U R U' R' U R U' R'

cases.name: Advanced — both in wrong slots
cases.setup: R U R' U' R U2 R' U' R U R'
cases.recognition:
FIND: The FR slot is either empty or has the wrong pieces. Check all four middle slots — both the corner and edge are somewhere in the middle layer, but NOT in the FR slot.
WHITE: Corner is in a middle slot, white not in the FR position.
EDGE: Edge is in a different middle slot.
CONFIRM: Both corner and edge in middle layer, neither in FR slot = f2l-38. The most complex standard case. If it looks close but not quite, verify which slots hold which pieces.

algorithms.label: Main
algorithms.moves: R U' R' U R U2 R' U R U' R'
algorithms.notes: Consider extracting one piece at a time as an alternative
